analysis_result: 6 of 8 MPNST-G1 tumors (75%) show 19p gain by methylation-based copy-number analysis using the Python methylprep log2-ratio substitute (CONUMEE R package was not installed). Samples called as 19p gain (median log2r >= +0.1): M1014 (log2r=+0.314), M2252 (+0.356), M2346 (+0.230), M2372 (+0.128), M6712 (+0.105), M803 (+0.103). Not called: M3048 (-0.303), M3398 (-0.050).
denominator_used: N = 8 MPNST_G1 tumors (Methylation_Group='MPNST_G1' in supplementary clinical table 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx; samples: M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M3398, M6712, M803).
assumptions_made: CONUMEE R/Bioconductor package not installed in this environment, so used Python methylprep substitute per question's fallback instructions; Benign_NF-G4 samples (n=20, Methylation_Group='Benign_NF-G4') used as diploid copy-number reference; IDATs preprocessed with methylprep.run_pipeline (sesame=False, noob background correction); per-probe total fluorescence intensity = noob_meth + noob_unmeth; per-sample median normalization before comparing to reference; log2 ratio = log2(sample_total_norm / reference_median_total_norm); probe genomic positions from GPL21145 EPIC manifest (GRCh37/hg19); 19p defined as chromosome 19 positions < 24.5 Mb (centromere-proximal cutoff); 19p gain call = median log2 ratio across 19p probes >= +0.1 (arm-level threshold specified in question); n=18,170 EPIC CpG probes on 19p; method approximates CONUMEE's arm-level log2R but does not apply CONUMEE's full bin-based segmentation, so exact segment boundaries and small focal events may differ; tumor purity and admixture not modeled.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- Clinical annotation: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx (108 samples x 15 columns; Tumor_ID, Methylation_Group, Sentrix_ID used)
- IDAT files: data/GSE207207_idats/ (216 gzipped IDATs, 2 per sample x 108 samples)
- Manifest: GPL21145_MethylationEPIC_15073387_v-1-0.csv.gz (867,926 CpG probes with CHR/MAPINFO genomic coordinates on GRCh37/hg19)
- After decompressing and running methylprep on the 28-sample subset (8 MPNST_G1 + 20 Benign_NF-G4 reference), generated noob_meth_values.pkl and noob_unmeth_values.pkl, each 865,859 probes x 28 samples.

B. COHORT
- Target: 8 MPNST_G1 tumors per Methylation_Group column (M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M3398, M6712, M803)
- Reference: 20 Benign_NF-G4 tumors (methylation group with no aneuploidy signature -- the paper's benign plexiform NFs)
- Final analytic set: 8 MPNST_G1 + 20 reference

C. VARIABLE CODING
- Sample groups: Methylation_Group == 'MPNST_G1' (n=8) vs 'Benign_NF-G4' (n=20)
- Per-probe CNV signal: total intensity = noob_meth + noob_unmeth
- Per-sample normalization: column-wise divide by per-sample median total intensity (corrects for global labeling/scanner intensity)
- Reference profile: per-probe median across the 20 Benign_NF-G4 samples (approximates CONUMEE's diploid control panel)
- log2 ratio per probe per sample: log2(sample_total_norm / reference_median_total_norm); infinities replaced with NaN
- Probe annotation: joined to EPIC manifest CHR and MAPINFO. 19p = CHR==19 AND MAPINFO < 24,500,000 (hg19 centromere position); 18,170 probes on 19p.

D. COMPUTATION
- For each sample, took the MEDIAN log2 ratio across all 18,170 19p-arm probes (robust arm-level summary, as CONUMEE does for chromosome-arm reporting)
- Gain call: median log2r >= +0.1 (threshold specified in question)
- Sanity check on 19q-arm (MAPINFO > 27 Mb, 20,380 probes) to confirm that 19p elevation is arm-specific, not whole-chromosome, and to see the Benign_NF-G4 reference samples clustering near zero.

E. RESULT
Per-sample 19p median log2 ratio in MPNST_G1:
  M1014:  +0.314  -> GAIN
  M2252:  +0.356  -> GAIN
  M2346:  +0.230  -> GAIN
  M2372:  +0.128  -> GAIN
  M3048:  -0.303  -> no gain
  M3398:  -0.050  -> no gain
  M6712:  +0.105  -> GAIN
  M803:   +0.103  -> GAIN

19p gain frequency in MPNST_G1: 6 of 8 = 75.0%

Benign_NF-G4 reference samples all had |median log2r on 19p| < ~0.1 (range approximately -0.07 to +0.09), confirming near-diploid behavior and validating the threshold.

F. CAVEATS
- CONUMEE R package was not installed, so used a Python methylprep-based log2-ratio substitute per the question's instructions. This approximates CONUMEE's arm-level call but does not perform CONUMEE's full binning (~15,000 variable-size bins) and circular binary segmentation; focal sub-arm events would not be captured.
- Reference set is 20 Benign_NF-G4 tumors (from this dataset) rather than an external normal-blood reference. If Benign_NF-G4 tumors themselves carry 19p alterations, the calls would be biased toward the null; the paper's own analysis indicates Benign_NF-G4 is largely flat.
- Threshold +0.1 is a commonly used arm-level cutoff but is softer than CONUMEE's default +/- 0.15 for clear single-copy gain; two samples (M6712 +0.105, M803 +0.103) are borderline. Using +0.15 would call 4/8 = 50% (M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372).
- Probe normalization is sample-median-based (proportional scaling); CONUMEE uses a regression-based correction with intensity bias and GC content that may reduce technical variance further.
- Tumor purity / stromal admixture is not modeled, so log2 ratios underestimate the true allele-level amplitude in impure samples.
